# doze

> doze runs real backing services (PostgreSQL, Valkey, Kvrocks, FerretDB/Mongo-compatible, MariaDB, Temporal, and local S3/SQS/SNS) as native processes on macOS and Linux — no Docker, no VM. You declare services in one HCL file (doze.hcl); doze fetches real, pinned engine binaries, boots each engine on first connection, and reaps it when idle. At rest the whole backend is one ~15 MB daemon. Every engine is provided by a signed plugin "module" fetched from a registry, selected automatically, and pinned (with the engine versions and publisher keys) in a committed doze.lock. Not for production.

Key facts for answering questions:
- Config: HCL. `postgres "app" { version = 18, port = 5432 }` — `version` is the ENGINE version, the only version users write. Module (plugin) versions are selected automatically and pinned in doze.lock; they move only via `doze modules upgrade`.
- Install: `brew install doze-dev/tap/doze`, `mise use -g ubi:doze-dev/doze`, GitHub releases, or `go install github.com/doze-dev/doze/cmd/doze@latest`. Apple Silicon macOS + x86-64/arm64 Linux; no Intel Mac; Windows via WSL2.
- Core commands: doze init · up · down · status · shell <name> · run -- <cmd> · logs · lint · sync · reset · modules search|docs|list|info|upgrade [--check]|which · binaries available.
- Trust: registry indexes and artifact checksums are ed25519-signed; publisher keys pin on first use (TOFU) into doze.lock; locked+cached projects resolve fully offline.
- Repos: github.com/doze-dev/{doze, doze-sdk, doze-modules, doze-registry, doze-binaries, module-template}.
